message:
feat(http2): implement RFC 7540 flow control with WINDOW_UPDATE emission

Add H2FlowControl struct that tracks connection-level and stream-level
receive windows per RFC 7540 Section 6.9. The server now sends
WINDOW_UPDATE frames when consumed data exceeds half the initial
window size, preventing sender stalling on large request bodies.

http2.rs:
- Add H2FlowControl with connection window tracking, consumed-byte
  accumulation, and configurable initial window size
- DEFAULT_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE (65,535 bytes) per RFC 7540
- Threshold-based update emission (half the window) balances
  throughput vs frame overhead
- 7 unit tests covering threshold behavior, accumulation, reset,
  stream-level updates, and custom window sizes

server.rs:
- Integrate H2FlowControl into all three HTTP/2 dispatch paths
  (App, Handler/TcpServer, and Closure/TcpServer)
- Track per-stream received bytes during body reads and emit both
  connection-level and stream-level WINDOW_UPDATEs via new
  send_window_updates() async helper
- Replace apply_http2_settings with apply_http2_settings_with_fc
  that forwards SETTINGS_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE (id 0x3) to the
  flow control tracker, with validation that value <= 2^31-1
- Add window_update_payload() helper for 4-byte frame construction

tests/http2.rs:
- Add 3 integration tests sending 80 KiB POST bodies (5x16 KiB
  DATA frames) and asserting that both connection-level and
  stream-level WINDOW_UPDATE frames are emitted, covering the
  App, Handler, and Closure server paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
